nodes.description: Gets H3 cells within a bounding box

nodes.description: Initializes the procedural generation client and loads the active recipe

nodes.name: getViewportObjects

nodes.name: setupDynamicLoading

nodes.name: Deterministic Generation with Seed
nodes.description: Architectural principle introduced to avoid saving procedural zones to Firebase, relying on reproducible seeds instead
nodes.rationale: Saves Firebase writes, simplifies branching between legacy and new procedural generation

nodes.name: Snapshot Recipe Configuration
nodes.description: A structured configuration defining object densities and templates for world generation
nodes.rationale: Allows admin to tweak world layout globally without running heavy batch jobs

nodes.name: Procedural Demo UI
nodes.description: User interface for testing and visualizing procedural generation on a map.

nodes.name: Generation Controls
nodes.description: Control panel with inputs for Seed, H3 Resolution, and ratios for Monsters, Shops, Castles, and Citadels.

nodes.name: Map View
nodes.description: Leaflet map displaying procedurally generated entities.

nodes.name: Entity Legend
nodes.description: Legend indicating colors for Monsters (Red), Shops (Green), Vaults (Purple), Castles (Orange), and Citadels (Blue).

nodes.name: Generation Stats
nodes.description: Shows counts of generated objects, H3 cells used, nearest city info, and generation time.

nodes.name: Debug Console
nodes.description: Logs procedural generation events, determinism verification, and loading states.

nodes.name: Deterministic Generation System
nodes.description: Procedural generation logic utilizing H3 cells, deterministic IDs, and population density.

properties.description: Screenshot of procedural generation demo showing map and UI controls for generating world objects based on H3 cells and a seed.
User interface for testing and configuring procedural generation with settings for seed, H3 resolution, and spawn ratios for different object types.
Leaflet map displaying procedurally generated entities like monsters, shops, vaults, castles, and citadels as colored markers.
Uses H3 hexagonal grid system (Resolution 8 shown) for world partitioning and procedural generation.
Engine that deterministically generates world objects (monsters, shops, etc.) based on H3 cells, seeds, and ratios, functioning in real-time on pan/zoom.

nodes.name: procedural-demo-screenshot.png
nodes.description: Screenshot of a procedural generation map demo interface for FightCraft, showing real-time deterministic object generation on a Leaflet map.

nodes.name: Interactive Map
nodes.description: Leaflet-based map displaying generated game objects (monsters, shops, etc.) on top of real-world geography.

nodes.name: Control Panel
nodes.description: UI panel containing inputs for procedural generation parameters: Seed, H3 Resolution, Monster Ratio, Shop Ratio, Castle Ratio, and Citadel Ratio. Includes buttons for Regenerate, Toggle H3 Cells, and Random Seed.

nodes.name: Status Bar
nodes.description: Displays real-time statistics: total objects, breakdown by type, H3 cells processed, nearest city, and generation time.

nodes.name: Event Log
nodes.description: Terminal-like console output showing generation events, timing, determinism verification, and system messages.

nodes.name: Map Legend
nodes.description: Legend indicating colors for map markers: Monsters (red), Shops (green), Vaults (purple), Castles (orange), Citadels (blue).

nodes.name: Procedural Generation
nodes.description: System for deterministically generating game objects based on location, seed, and H3 cells.

nodes.name: H3 Spatial Index
nodes.description: Hexagonal hierarchical spatial index used for world partitioning and procedural generation.

nodes.name: procedural-demo-zones.png
nodes.description: Screenshot of procedural world generation demo with Leaflet map, H3 zones logging, and UI controls for generation ratios.

nodes.name: seed
nodes.description: Seed parameter (e.g. 42) for deterministic procedural world generation.

nodes.name: h3_zones
nodes.description: Hexagonal spatial indexing zones shown in the generation log.

nodes.name: generation_ratios
nodes.description: Parameters for monster, shop, castle, and citadel generation ratios.

nodes.name: procedural-demo-zoomed-out.png
nodes.description: Screenshot of a web-based procedural generation demo tool for FightCraft, showing a zoomed-out Leaflet map densely populated with generated objects like monsters, shops, vaults, castles, and citadels. Features a control panel for seed, H3 resolution (e.g., 8), and entity ratios, plus statistics showing generation times (e.g., ~434ms for ~200k objects) and determinism verification.

nodes.name: procedural-world-preview.png
nodes.description: Screenshot of a procedural world generation preview tool displaying a global map with clustered citadels. Features controls for generation parameters (seed, ratios for monsters, shops, castles, citadels) and a console log detailing Level of Detail (LOD) rendering rules.

nodes.name: Procedural World Generation UI
nodes.description: An interface for previewing procedural generation of game world entities (citadels, castles, shops, monsters) based on seed and density ratios.

nodes.name: Zoom-based LOD Rendering
nodes.description: A rendering optimization technique described in the preview tool's console where entity visibility depends on map zoom level: citadels (zoom 1-7), castles (8-10), shops/vaults (11-13), and monsters (14+).

nodes.label: Canvas Rendering Pattern
nodes.rationale: Optimizing map rendering by drawing thousands of entities directly on HTML5 Canvas overlaid on Leaflet instead of DOM elements, maintaining interactive performance.

nodes.label: Leading + Trailing Edge Sync
nodes.rationale: Player position is synced to RTDB immediately on first move (leading edge), then throttled, but guaranteed to send the final position (trailing edge) ensuring the latest location is not lost during fast movements.

nodes.label: Hexagonal Grid Overlay
nodes.rationale: Shows a dense H3-style hexagonal grid clustered by color over the map to partition territories.

nodes.label: Voronoi Zones
nodes.rationale: Used for boundary generation and grouping hexagons into distinct regions or city zones, as seen in the console logs.

nodes.label: Seed-Based World Generation
nodes.rationale: UI provides inputs for Seed, Monster Ratio, Shop Ratio, Castle Ratio, and Citadel Ratio to procedurally generate the world entities.

nodes.label: Leaflet Map Base
nodes.rationale: The underlying map renderer is Leaflet using OpenStreetMap tiles with a dark theme.

links.notes: Both functions serve the exact same purpose in different test files
Both test helpers generate mock snapshot recipes for validation
Deterministic generation concept relies on the world seed derivation
getObjectsForViewport orchestrates cell generation by mapping over viewport cells
Renderer delegates to core procedural engine for fetching cell data
Client uses renderer to generate entities within the bounds
Initialization loads the active snapshot recipe
Dynamic loading interacts with the procedural client to fetch viewable objects
The concept documented matches the implementation in the snapshot recipe logic
Map initialization sets up dynamic loading listeners

links.description: Generation system relies on UI controls for parameters.
Generation system outputs entities to the map view.
Debug console logs events from the generation system.

properties.evidence: Image visually contains the UI elements.
Image visually contains the map with markers.
UI has inputs for seed, ratios, and a regenerate button, implying it controls the generation engine.
Map shows the output markers from the generation engine.
UI and console logs explicitly mention H3 resolution and cells (e.g., '24 H3 cells').

links.description: The image captures the interface of the world generation preview tool.
The image's console log explicitly defines the zoom-based LOD rendering thresholds.
